headline: Up 550% YTD, SanDisk Stock at $1,540: Buy, Sell or Hold?
summary: At $1,540, SanDisk (NASDAQ:SNDK) is a hold. After a parabolic 549.69% year-to-date move, the fundamentals look spectacular but the entry point demands discipline. SanDisk is the pure-play NAND flash vendor spun out of Western Digital in February 2025, manufacturing solid-state drives, embedded storage, and memory cards through its Kioxia joint venture extended through December 2034. ... Up 550% YTD, SanDisk Stock at $1,540: Buy, Sell or Hold?

headline: Can Seagate Sustain Its Gross Margin Expansion Momentum?
summary: STX hits record 47% gross margin on $3.1B Q3 revenue as HAMR Mozaic drives, tight supply and FY27 pricing deals lift cash flow.

headline: Western Digital PQC Hard Drives Put Long Term Data Security In Focus
summary: Western Digital (NasdaqGS:WDC) has introduced Ultrastar UltraSMR enterprise hard drives that integrate post-quantum cryptography. The drives are described as the first production storage devices to use NIST approved quantum resistant algorithms. The launch targets hyperscale and enterprise storage customers focused on long term protection of AI and other sensitive data. For Western Digital, a major supplier of hard drives and flash storage, this move speaks directly to how data security is...

headline: DRAM, MU, SNDK: US Memory Stocks Pare Gains After Boost From Samsung Averting Major Worker Strike
summary: The Roundhill Memory ETF (DRAM) declined 1.5% in Friday’s premarket session, tracking a 2.3% drop in Samsung shares in Seoul on Friday.
